bugs.summary: The patch removes the Release-time call that stops snapshot generation before diskLayer resources are released.
bugs.impact: During shutdown, diskLayer.Release can reset resources without first stopping the background snapshot generator, allowing the generator goroutine to survive cleanup and leak or access closed database state.
acceptableFixes: Have diskLayer.Release call stopGeneration before releasing/resetting resources.
Ensure Release is a lifecycle barrier that stops and waits for the snapshot generator before database/cache cleanup can proceed.

bugs.summary: The patch reintroduces an unbuffered genAbort lifecycle protocol that can park a completed generator waiting for an external abort and deadlock later callers after the receiver exits.
bugs.impact: Snapshot lifecycle operations such as Journal or diffToDisk can block forever because they send on genAbort based only on a non-nil channel, not on a live receiver or done state.

requiredEvidence.quote: Someone will be looking for us, wait it out

acceptableFixes: Use an idempotent cancel/done lifecycle instead of an unbuffered genAbort channel that remains non-nil after the receiver exits.
Clear or close genAbort under synchronization when generation exits, and ensure Journal/diffToDisk/stop paths cannot send without a live receiver.
Keep Release/stopGeneration blocking until the generator goroutine exits, including after generation has completed but before cleanup is acknowledged.

mustAvoidClaims: Using upstream PR titles, issue numbers, commit messages, or git history as evidence.
Relying on private repro.sh, gold-key.draft.json, or reference-fix.patch.
Claiming unrelated broad rewrites outside the proposed patch are required.
Treating geth-case-007 as a primary gate before masking candidate-visible regression tests/comments or choosing an earlier source revision.
Double-counting one vague generator lifecycle finding as both G1 and G2 without distinguishing Release cleanup from genAbort repeated-send/deadlock.

difficulty.rationale: The bug is a shutdown lifecycle race involving generator completion state, Release, and goroutine cleanup rather than a single local condition.

candidateVisible: sanitized go-ethereum workspace at pinned revision
candidateForbidden: judge prompts, answer keys, run artifacts, and A/B mappings
upstream PR/issue text or commit message

approvedBy: source-only reverse fixture with RED/GREEN repro
local gold review: geth-case-007 genAbort lifecycle issue added as G2
approval.notes: GPT-5.5 high provider review returned NEEDS_CHANGES: G1 and G2 are real/separable, but source revision leaks intended issues through regression test/comments; keep draft until masked/narrowed or provider-approved.
